title: Production File Delivery with Amazon S3 and CloudFront in Go 1.26
description: Go backend developers who can defend production HTTP services and need to build, secure, test, operate, and recover high-volume file and media delivery with Amazon S3 and CloudFront. This original, research-backed path uses repeated retrieval, cumulative artifacts, changed-case labs, stakeholder projects, and a performance exam.
outcomes: Demonstrate go 1.26.5, aws sdk for go v2 1.42.1, s3 1.105.0, transfermanager 0.3.2, cloudfront/sign 1.11.7, http file semantics, s3 object and bucket models, sdk clients, credentials, retries, bounded upload and download, integrity, multipart, direct browser upload, go gateways, metadata transactions, versioning, object lock, lifecycle, iam, encryption, signed access, oac, cloudfront caching, media ranges, edge security, replication, recovery, events, observability, capacity, cost, tests, faults, and release defense through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate runnable deterministic pure-go storage decision functions plus explicit go compile, vet, race, fuzz, leak, controlled aws integration, load, security, cost, restore, and production transfer gates through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate five cumulative real-world file-delivery systems and a performance-based production defense examination through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
sharedRequirements: Every activity retrieves Go, HTTP semantics, accessibility, security, validation, persistence, testing, context, goroutine ownership, resource budgets, and evidence habits before adding one storage or delivery boundary.

Browser Go is deterministic and isolated. It models identity, ranges, policy, integrity, lifecycle, caching, cost, and recovery decisions but never imports AWS SDK packages, opens sockets, contacts AWS or databases, reads host state, executes commands, uses credentials, or causes external effects.

Passing work requires stable file and scenario identity, a named owner and state, bounded bytes, time, attempts, concurrency, cache and cost, observable changed and failure results, cleanup and recovery, and explicit compiler, controlled AWS, security, load, cost, restore, or production transfer limits.

competencies.statement: Define file-delivery success with learner, uploader, viewer, owner, security, accessibility, operations, and finance evidence.
misconceptions: A 200 response and an S3 object key prove the stakeholder outcome.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define file-delivery success with learner, uploader, viewer, owner, security, accessibility, operations, and finance evidence.

competencies.statement: Trace browser, Go service, object store, CDN, identity provider, scanner, database, and stakeholder custody without assigning evidence a boundary cannot observe.
misconceptions: Once a request body reaches the Go handler, the file is durably stored and usable.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace browser, Go service, object store, CDN, identity provider, scanner, database, and stakeholder custody without assigning evidence a boundary cannot observe.

competencies.statement: Separate business file identity, object key, object version, representation bytes, metadata, checksum, URL, and cache entry.
misconceptions: A filename, S3 key, URL, and file identity are interchangeable.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate business file identity, object key, object version, representation bytes, metadata, checksum, URL, and cache entry.

competencies.statement: Set observable availability, latency, integrity, freshness, recovery, accessibility, privacy, and cost targets with measurement windows.

competencies.statement: Plan deterministic browser models, Go compile and race gates, disposable integration, controlled AWS tests, load, fault, restore, security, cost, and production evidence.

misconceptions: DELETE always erases the object bytes immediately and permanently.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish unversioned deletion, current-version delete markers, version-specific deletion, retention, legal hold, and recovery.

competencies.statement: Chunk batch operations, correlate per-object outcomes, retry only eligible items, preserve authorization, and report partial success accessibly.
misconceptions: One successful batch HTTP response means every requested object changed.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Chunk batch operations, correlate per-object outcomes, retry only eligible items, preserve authorization, and report partial success accessibly.

competencies.statement: Distinguish unversioned, enabled, and suspended versioning and test null versions, overwrite behavior, and lifecycle impact.
misconceptions: Suspending versioning returns a bucket to its original unversioned behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish unversioned, enabled, and suspended versioning and test null versions, overwrite behavior, and lifecycle impact.

competencies.statement: List versions and delete markers, resolve current reads, restore by removing a marker or copying a version, and preserve audit identity.
misconceptions: A delete marker contains a recoverable copy of the deleted bytes.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: List versions and delete markers, resolve current reads, restore by removing a marker or copying a version, and preserve audit identity.

competencies.statement: Apply governance, compliance, legal hold, retention date, and authorized bypass by version with irreversible-risk review.
misconceptions: Object Lock prevents new versions and delete markers from being created.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Apply governance, compliance, legal hold, retention date, and authorized bypass by version with irreversible-risk review.

competencies.statement: Separate WORM retention, version recovery, replication, independent backup, restore testing, and account-compromise boundaries.
misconceptions: Versioning plus Object Lock is a complete independent backup strategy.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate WORM retention, version recovery, replication, independent backup, restore testing, and account-compromise boundaries.

competencies.statement: Run sampled restore drills with authorization, known bytes, metadata, dependencies, time objectives, audit evidence, and safe cleanup.
misconceptions: A documented restore command proves recovery without executing a drill.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Run sampled restore drills with authorization, known bytes, metadata, dependencies, time objectives, audit evidence, and safe cleanup.

competencies.statement: Select storage class from access frequency, object size, retrieval latency, resilience, minimum duration, monitoring, and transition cost.
misconceptions: The lowest per-gigabyte storage class is always cheapest.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Select storage class from access frequency, object size, retrieval latency, resilience, minimum duration, monitoring, and transition cost.

competencies.statement: Model filters, current and noncurrent transitions, expiration, delete markers, multipart cleanup, precedence, propagation, and unsupported bucket features.
misconceptions: Lifecycle rules run immediately and in authored order like a script.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model filters, current and noncurrent transitions, expiration, delete markers, multipart cleanup, precedence, propagation, and unsupported bucket features.

competencies.statement: Request, monitor, authorize, and communicate temporary archive restoration with tier, expiry, duplicate request, and failure states.
misconceptions: Restoring an archived object permanently changes its storage class.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Request, monitor, authorize, and communicate temporary archive restoration with tier, expiry, duplicate request, and failure states.

competencies.statement: Resolve lifecycle expiration against versioning, Object Lock, legal hold, replication, business retention, and deletion evidence.
misconceptions: An expiration rule overrides Object Lock when storage cost is high.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Resolve lifecycle expiration against versioning, Object Lock, legal hold, replication, business retention, and deletion evidence.

competencies.statement: Calculate storage, requests, transitions, retrieval, early deletion, transfer, CDN, invalidation, logs, and operational labor under changed traffic.
misconceptions: Cost estimation can ignore failure retries and cache-miss behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Calculate storage, requests, transitions, retrieval, early deletion, transfer, CDN, invalidation, logs, and operational labor under changed traffic.

competencies.statement: Evaluate identity and resource policies by principal, action, exact resource, condition, session, explicit deny, and organization boundary.
misconceptions: An IAM allow always wins over a bucket-policy deny.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Evaluate identity and resource policies by principal, action, exact resource, condition, session, explicit deny, and organization boundary.

competencies.statement: Enforce Block Public Access and bucket-owner-enforced Object Ownership while explaining what each does and does not authorize.
misconceptions: Block Public Access makes all bucket access private and least-privileged.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Enforce Block Public Access and bucket-owner-enforced Object Ownership while explaining what each does and does not authorize.

competencies.statement: Derive tenant-safe keys and list prefixes server-side and bind allowed actions and conditions without trusting user-supplied policy fragments.
misconceptions: A key prefix containing the tenant ID is an authorization boundary by itself.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Derive tenant-safe keys and list prefixes server-side and bind allowed actions and conditions without trusting user-supplied policy fragments.

competencies.statement: Use access points and VPC restrictions to separate application or dataset access while retaining bucket-policy and network evidence.
misconceptions: An access point creates a separate copy of the bucket data and policies.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use access points and VPC restrictions to separate application or dataset access while retaining bucket-policy and network evidence.

competencies.statement: Review effective permissions with policy analysis, simulation, denied changed cases, temporary credentials, and CloudTrail evidence.
misconceptions: One successful authorized request proves there is no broader unintended access.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Review effective permissions with policy analysis, simulation, denied changed cases, temporary credentials, and CloudTrail evidence.

competencies.statement: Separate TLS in transit, S3 server-side encryption, KMS envelope encryption, and application client-side encryption by threat and feature tradeoff.
misconceptions: SSE-KMS keeps plaintext hidden from the application and S3 service.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate TLS in transit, S3 server-side encryption, KMS envelope encryption, and application client-side encryption by threat and feature tradeoff.

competencies.statement: Choose SSE-S3, SSE-KMS, dual-layer KMS where required, or client encryption from audit, tenancy, performance, cost, and service integration needs.
misconceptions: SSE-KMS is automatically the safest and cheapest choice for every object.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose SSE-S3, SSE-KMS, dual-layer KMS where required, or client encryption from audit, tenancy, performance, cost, and service integration needs.

competencies.statement: Constrain IAM and KMS key policy, grants, service principal, encryption context, region, rotation, disable, deletion, and break-glass ownership.
misconceptions: IAM permission to an S3 object automatically grants use of its KMS key.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Constrain IAM and KMS key policy, grants, service principal, encryption context, region, rotation, disable, deletion, and break-glass ownership.

competencies.statement: Evaluate S3 Bucket Keys for KMS request cost while documenting encryption-context and audit changes.
misconceptions: Bucket Keys change only billing and never authorization or audit semantics.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Evaluate S3 Bucket Keys for KMS request cost while documenting encryption-context and audit changes.

competencies.statement: Prove new writes, old reads, role rotation, key disable recovery, compromised-session revocation limits, and residual cached or signed access.
misconceptions: Rotating a KMS key immediately re-encrypts every existing object and revokes every URL.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Prove new writes, old reads, role rotation, key disable recovery, compromised-session revocation limits, and residual cached or signed access.

competencies.statement: Presign the exact version, response metadata, method, region, credential scope, and shortest useful expiry while treating the URL as a bearer secret.
misconceptions: A presigned S3 URL has permissions independent from the signing principal.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Presign the exact version, response metadata, method, region, credential scope, and shortest useful expiry while treating the URL as a bearer secret.

competencies.statement: Choose signed URL or signed cookie by object count, path scope, player behavior, client constraints, and leakage surface.
misconceptions: Signed cookies are always more secure because the URL contains no signature.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose signed URL or signed cookie by object count, path scope, player behavior, client constraints, and leakage surface.

competencies.statement: Choose canned or custom CloudFront policy and constrain expiry, optional start time, resource wildcard, and IP range without accidental overbreadth.
misconceptions: A wildcard resource in a custom policy is equivalent to signing one exact object.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose canned or custom CloudFront policy and constrain expiry, optional start time, resource wildcard, and IP range without accidental overbreadth.

competencies.statement: Use CloudFront sign 1.11.7 with reviewed RSA or ECDSA keys and explicit HashSHA256 support while preserving compatibility evidence for zero-value SHA-1 behavior.
misconceptions: Selecting a modern Go crypto default automatically changes every CloudFront signature to SHA-256.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use CloudFront sign 1.11.7 with reviewed RSA or ECDSA keys and explicit HashSHA256 support while preserving compatibility evidence for zero-value SHA-1 behavior.

competencies.statement: Model expiry-at-request-start, range continuation, key-group rotation, authorization changes, logs and referrers, cache behavior, and emergency revocation limits.
misconceptions: Deleting an application session immediately revokes every issued S3 or CloudFront URL.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model expiry-at-request-start, range continuation, key-group rotation, authorization changes, logs and referrers, cache behavior, and emergency revocation limits.

competencies.statement: Distinguish regular S3 origins from website endpoints by HTTPS, OAC, redirects, error pages, methods, and public-access requirements.
misconceptions: An S3 website endpoint can remain private behind OAC.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish regular S3 origins from website endpoints by HTTPS, OAC, redirects, error pages, methods, and public-access requirements.

competencies.statement: Configure OAC with always-sign where appropriate and define viewer protocol, origin protocol, request signing, and forwarded Authorization behavior.
misconceptions: OAC authenticates viewers as well as CloudFront to the S3 origin.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Configure OAC with always-sign where appropriate and define viewer protocol, origin protocol, request signing, and forwarded Authorization behavior.

competencies.statement: Scope the CloudFront service principal to the exact distribution SourceArn and required object actions without public or cross-distribution access.
misconceptions: Allowing cloudfront.amazonaws.com in a bucket policy is sufficiently narrow by default.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Scope the CloudFront service principal to the exact distribution SourceArn and required object actions without public or cross-distribution access.

competencies.statement: Grant the exact distribution use of the KMS key separately from S3 bucket access and verify encrypted read and write cases.
misconceptions: A correct S3 bucket policy is enough for CloudFront to read SSE-KMS objects.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Grant the exact distribution use of the KMS key separately from S3 bucket access and verify encrypted read and write cases.

competencies.statement: Migrate OAI to OAC with temporary dual authorization, deployed distribution evidence, negative tests, monitoring, and policy rollback.
misconceptions: Replacing the origin identity ID in one policy statement is a zero-risk migration.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Migrate OAI to OAC with temporary dual authorization, deployed distribution evidence, negative tests, monitoring, and policy rollback.

competencies.statement: Include only representation-changing path, query, header, cookie, encoding, and authorization dimensions in the cache key.
misconceptions: Forwarding a value to the origin automatically places it in the cache key.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Include only representation-changing path, query, header, cookie, encoding, and authorization dimensions in the cache key.

competencies.statement: Use origin request policy for additional required viewer data without unnecessary cache fragmentation or trust escalation.
misconceptions: Cache policy and origin request policy are two names for the same configuration.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use origin request policy for additional required viewer data without unnecessary cache fragmentation or trust escalation.

competencies.statement: Combine origin Cache-Control, minimum, default, maximum, stale behavior, validators, and error caching with content-change and privacy requirements.
misconceptions: A short default TTL guarantees private or changed content cannot be served stale.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Combine origin Cache-Control, minimum, default, maximum, stale behavior, validators, and error caching with content-change and privacy requirements.

competencies.statement: Prefer content-versioned names for routine immutable releases and reserve path or cache-tag invalidation for bounded urgent purge with completion evidence.
misconceptions: Invalidating /* is the safest and cheapest deployment strategy.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Prefer content-versioned names for routine immutable releases and reserve path or cache-tag invalidation for bounded urgent purge with completion evidence.

competencies.statement: Configure Brotli or gzip variants, Accept-Encoding cache behavior, already-compressed media, content length, and changed client capability evidence.
misconceptions: CloudFront compression reduces every file type and never changes cache identity.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Configure Brotli or gzip variants, Accept-Encoding cache behavior, already-compressed media, content length, and changed client capability evidence.

competencies.statement: Meet ascending, nonoverlapping, valid Range behavior, understand larger origin fetches, 200 fallbacks, chunked-origin limits, and cache chunking.
misconceptions: CloudFront forwards every Range byte boundary unchanged and always returns 206.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Meet ascending, nonoverlapping, valid Range behavior, understand larger origin fetches, 200 fallbacks, chunked-origin limits, and cache chunking.

competencies.statement: Prepare correct media type, length, validators, byte ranges, and fast-start MP4 metadata for progressive download and seeking.
misconceptions: Serving video/mp4 with Accept-Ranges automatically makes every MP4 seek efficiently.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Prepare correct media type, length, validators, byte ranges, and fast-start MP4 metadata for progressive download and seeking.

competencies.statement: Model manifests, variant playlists, segments, codecs, durations, naming, cache lifetimes, and atomic publication for adaptive delivery.
misconceptions: HLS or DASH is one continuously growing S3 object.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model manifests, variant playlists, segments, codecs, durations, naming, cache lifetimes, and atomic publication for adaptive delivery.

competencies.statement: Align manifest and segment authorization, signed cookies or URLs, CORS, key delivery, expiry, seeking, and leakage boundaries.
misconceptions: Protecting the manifest URL alone keeps its media segments private.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Align manifest and segment authorization, signed cookies or URLs, CORS, key delivery, expiry, seeking, and leakage boundaries.

competencies.statement: Provide captions, transcripts, audio description where needed, player keyboard operation, status, quality fallback, and representative network tests.
misconceptions: CDN throughput is the only quality metric that matters to media users.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Provide captions, transcripts, audio description where needed, player keyboard operation, status, quality fallback, and representative network tests.

competencies.statement: Apply WAF, rate limits, bot controls, request size and method rules with false-positive, cost, accessibility, and bypass evidence.
misconceptions: A WAF managed ruleset replaces application authorization and upload validation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Apply WAF, rate limits, bot controls, request size and method rules with false-positive, cost, accessibility, and bypass evidence.

competencies.statement: Treat Referer hotlink controls as a weak cost signal and use signed access or a controlled origin header only within its actual threat boundary.
misconceptions: Blocking unknown Referer values securely prevents content theft.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Treat Referer hotlink controls as a weak cost signal and use signed access or a controlled origin header only within its actual threat boundary.

competencies.statement: Set HSTS, MIME sniffing, frame, referrer, content security, cross-origin, and content-disposition headers appropriate to file types and embedding.
misconceptions: One generic security-header policy is correct for downloads, media, and embeddable assets.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Set HSTS, MIME sniffing, frame, referrer, content security, cross-origin, and content-disposition headers appropriate to file types and embedding.

competencies.statement: Use geographic restrictions only with documented business, legal, accuracy, accessibility, VPN, and privacy limits.
misconceptions: CloudFront country detection proves a viewer legal residence or identity.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use geographic restrictions only with documented business, legal, accuracy, accessibility, VPN, and privacy limits.

competencies.statement: Use Origin Shield, request collapsing, failover, bounded error caching, stale delivery, and load shedding without hiding recovery or serving private content.
misconceptions: Long error-cache TTL always protects the origin safely during incidents.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use Origin Shield, request collapsing, failover, bounded error caching, stale delivery, and load shedding without hiding recovery or serving private content.

competencies.statement: Separate object durability, request availability, regional reachability, accidental deletion, corruption, account compromise, and application recovery.
misconceptions: Eleven-nines durability guarantees the application can recover every file immediately.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate object durability, request availability, regional reachability, accidental deletion, corruption, account compromise, and application recovery.

competencies.statement: Configure versioning, destination ownership, IAM, KMS, filters, delete-marker behavior, replication status, metrics, and failure remediation.
misconceptions: Cross-Region Replication copies all existing objects and every delete automatically.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Configure versioning, destination ownership, IAM, KMS, filters, delete-marker behavior, replication status, metrics, and failure remediation.

competencies.statement: Evaluate Multi-Region Access Points, latency routing, active-active writes, consistency, replication lag, failover controls, and client endpoint support.
misconceptions: A multi-region endpoint creates synchronous cross-region writes with zero conflicts.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Evaluate Multi-Region Access Points, latency routing, active-active writes, consistency, replication lag, failover controls, and client endpoint support.

competencies.statement: Set RPO and RTO per failure and data class and connect them to replication, backup isolation, detection, decision, restore, and validation evidence.
misconceptions: One RPO and RTO pair covers deletion, corruption, region loss, and account compromise equally.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Set RPO and RTO per failure and data class and connect them to replication, backup isolation, detection, decision, restore, and validation evidence.

competencies.statement: Run controlled region, credential, deletion, corruption, dependency, and restore drills with abort, stakeholder communication, integrity checks, and lessons.
misconceptions: A replication dashboard in a healthy region proves disaster recovery.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Run controlled region, credential, deletion, corruption, dependency, and restore drills with abort, stakeholder communication, integrity checks, and lessons.

competencies.statement: Consume S3 notifications through supported destinations with duplicate, ordering, filtering, recursion, schema, delay, and lost-consumer recovery limits.
misconceptions: S3 event notifications are exactly once and globally ordered by key.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Consume S3 notifications through supported destinations with duplicate, ordering, filtering, recursion, schema, delay, and lost-consumer recovery limits.

competencies.statement: Correlate application operation, AWS request IDs, CloudTrail data events where justified, access logs, CloudFront logs, and stakeholder result without secrets.
misconceptions: One distributed trace contains every authoritative AWS audit fact.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Correlate application operation, AWS request IDs, CloudTrail data events where justified, access logs, CloudFront logs, and stakeholder result without secrets.

competencies.statement: Measure admitted and completed bytes, latency, failures, cache hit, range behavior, retries, backlog, replication, restore, and spend with bounded labels.
misconceptions: Object key and user ID are safe metric labels because they improve debugging.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Measure admitted and completed bytes, latency, failures, cache hit, range behavior, retries, backlog, replication, restore, and spend with bounded labels.

competencies.statement: Use S3 Inventory, Storage Lens, analytics, and sampled reconciliation for object estate, encryption, replication, lifecycle, ownership, and cost evidence.
misconceptions: A live ListObjects scan is always cheaper and more complete than inventory reports.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use S3 Inventory, Storage Lens, analytics, and sampled reconciliation for object estate, encryption, replication, lifecycle, ownership, and cost evidence.

competencies.statement: Forecast request, byte, concurrency, part, KMS, origin, egress, log, and budget limits and connect alerts to admission, degradation, and owner actions.
misconceptions: S3 scales automatically, so application capacity and cost alarms are unnecessary.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Forecast request, byte, concurrency, part, KMS, origin, egress, log, and budget limits and connect alerts to admission, degradation, and owner actions.

competencies.statement: Use table, property, and fuzz tests for key, range, policy, lifecycle, state-machine, checksum, retry, and cache invariants with changed cases.
misconceptions: High line coverage proves file-delivery behavior under boundary inputs.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use table, property, and fuzz tests for key, range, policy, lifecycle, state-machine, checksum, retry, and cache invariants with changed cases.

competencies.statement: Use fakes and emulators for deterministic feedback while listing unsupported AWS semantics and assigning controlled integration tests.
misconceptions: A local S3-compatible emulator proves IAM, KMS, OAC, CloudFront, replication, and billing behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use fakes and emulators for deterministic feedback while listing unsupported AWS semantics and assigning controlled integration tests.

competencies.statement: Gate formatting, vet, static analysis, tests, fuzz seeds, race, goroutine and body leaks, benchmarks, profiles, reproducible build, and dependency review.
misconceptions: Passing go test once proves race freedom and resource cleanup.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Gate formatting, vet, static analysis, tests, fuzz seeds, race, goroutine and body leaks, benchmarks, profiles, reproducible build, and dependency review.

competencies.statement: Run least-privilege controlled AWS tests for integrity, multipart ambiguity, throttling, cancellation, cache, signatures, WAF, KMS, replication, restore, load, and cost caps.
misconceptions: A production smoke upload is sufficient integration and resilience evidence.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Run least-privilege controlled AWS tests for integrity, multipart ambiguity, throttling, cancellation, cache, signatures, WAF, KMS, replication, restore, load, and cost caps.

competencies.statement: Canary immutable artifacts and infrastructure, compare changed behavior and cost, preserve rollback data and policies, rehearse recovery, and defend residual risk.
misconceptions: Rolling back Go code automatically restores overwritten objects, expired URLs, cache state, IAM, KMS, and lifecycle policy.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Canary immutable artifacts and infrastructure, compare changed behavior and cost, preserve rollback data and policies, rehearse recovery, and defend residual risk.

credential.title: Production File Delivery with Amazon S3 and CloudFront in Go 1.26 Certificate
